rulings.Case Title: PATRICK HELLAND VS. RAY BRONNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 5, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT RAY BRONNER'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. DENIED. IT IS A RECOGNIZED PLEADING METHOD TO INCORPORATE ALLEGATIONS BY REFERENCE. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS DID NOT NEED TO BE RAISED IN THE LOCKS CASE BECAUSE THE LOCKS CASE INVOLVED A DIFFERENT TRANSACTION AND PLAINTIFF NEVER FILED AN ANSWER. SEE CCP SECTION 426.30(b)(2). DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE IS UNTIMELY. CCP SECTION 435(b)(1). THE BACK RENT CLAIM COULD NOT BE RAISED IN THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER CASE THAT WAS BASED ON NUISANCE. PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ADRIAN FOX VS. NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 5, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECTS CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. CLAIMED INJURY OCCURRED OUTSIDE OF WARRANTY PERIOD. PLAINTIFFS DO NOT ADEQUATELY ALLEGE A SERIOUS PERSONAL SAFETY RISK TO ANY OF THE NAMED PLAINTIFFS OR IDENTIFIABLE CLASS MEMBERS. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO ESTABLISH A DUTY TO DISCLOSE THE ALLEGED DEFECT, RELIANCE, OR A VIOLATION OF LAW. THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT IDENTIFY ANY MISREPRESENTATIONS MADE ABOUT THE POWER VALVE SCREWS, OR AN OMMISSION CONTRARY TO AN ACTUAL REPRESENTATION MADE BY DEFENDANT. NOTHING IN THE CITED ADVERTISING CAMPAIGN RELATES DIRECTLY TO THE ALLEGED DEFECT, AND THE REPRESENTATIONS CONSTITUTE MERE "PUFFERY." FAILURE TO DISCLOSE A DEFECT THAT, MIGHT OR MIGHT NOT, SHORTEN THE EFFECTIVE LIFE SPAN OF AN AUTOMOBILIE/PART THAT FUNCTIONS PRECISELY AS WARRANTED THROUGHOUT THE TERM OF ITS EXPRESS WARRANTY CANNOT BE CHARACTERIZED AS CAUSING A SUBSTANTIAL INJURY TO CONSUMERS. SEE DAUGHERTY V. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC. (2006) 144 CAL.APP.4TH 824. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GMAC MORTGAGE LLC FKA GMAC MORTGAGE CORPORATION VS. FLORENTINO VILLANUEVA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 5, 2010, line 10, DEFENDANT FLORENTINO VILLANUEVA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER CONTENDS THAT THE INSTANT UNLAWFUL DETAINER AND CASE #501444, WHICH ALLEGES A WRONGFUL FORECLOSURE AND VIOLATION OF THE UCL, INVOLVE THE SAME CAUSES OF ACTION AND SAME PARTIES. THAT ARGUMENT FAILS AS A MATTER OF LAW. EACH CASE INVOLVES DIFFERENT CAUSES OF ACTION. SEE BUSH V. SUPERIOR COURT, (1992) 10 CAL.APP.4TH 1374, 1384. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GMAC MORTGAGE LLC FKA GMAC MORTGAGE CORPORATION VS. FLORENTINO VILLANUEVA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-10-501444 WITH CASE CUD-10-633822
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 5, 2010, line 10, DEFENDANT FLORENTINO VILLANUEVA'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-10-501444 WITH CASE CUD-10-633822. DENIED. AN UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION IS A SUMMARY PROCEEDING. DEFENDANT MAY SEEK A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION TO STAY THE EVICTION PROCEEDINGS UNTIL TRIAL OF DEFENDANT'S WRONGFUL FORECLOSURE ACTION PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 526. SEE ASUNCION V. THE SUPERIOR COURT OF SAN DIEGO COUNTY (1980) 108 CAL.APP. 3D 141, 146. =(302/CWW)
